overview.problem: Golaris has four critical multi-replica correctness bugs: (1) scheduler loops exit early with return instead of continue when subscription nil, skipping remaining entries; (2) local subscriptionCancelMap provides cancellation signal invisible to other replicas; (3) listener sets cancel status without checking entry existence, creating race condition; (4) handler/republishing locks use TryLockWithTimeout without lease, orphaning locks on process crash until session timeout.
overview.approach: Four independent milestones targeting each bug: M-001 fixes return-vs-continue in scheduler loops; M-002 removes local cancel map and replaces with distributed Hazelcast ContainsKey checks with per-batch granularity; M-003 adds missing entry check before cancel signal in listener; M-004 replaces all TryLockWithTimeout with TryLockWithLeaseAndTimeout (60s lease). Each milestone includes unit/integration tests using existing dockertest+testify patterns.
overview.title: Fix Multi-Replica Correctness in Golaris Scheduler and Cancellation

decisions.decision: Replace return with continue in scheduler loops
decisions.reasoning: scheduler.go:98,131 use return when subscription nil -> exits entire loop -> skips remaining entries. continue allows processing remaining entries in same iteration.

decisions.decision: Remove local subscriptionCancelMap and use Hazelcast ContainsKey for distributed cancellation
decisions.reasoning: Local map (cache.go:47-66) invisible across replicas -> goroutines on other instances miss cancellation signals. Hazelcast entry existence provides distributed signal visible to all replicas.

decisions.decision: Add RepublishingCache entry existence check before setting cancel status in listener.go:102-105
decisions.reasoning: handleDeliveryTypeChangeFromSSEToCallback sets cancel=true (listener.go:119) but missing entry check -> race if goroutine deletes entry between check and set. Entry check prevents false cancellation signal.

decisions.decision: Replace TryLockWithTimeout with TryLockWithLeaseAndTimeout (60s lease) for handler and republishing locks
decisions.reasoning: Process crash while holding lock orphans lock until Hazelcast session timeout (~minutes). Lease-based locks auto-release after 60s, matching HealthCheckCache pattern (healthcheck.go:154).

decisions.decision: Add context.Context parameter to RepublishPendingEvents signature
decisions.reasoning: ContainsKey requires context.Context but RepublishPendingEvents currently lacks context parameter. Signature change enables distributed cancel checks.

decisions.decision: Check ContainsKey per-batch (before batch processing) rather than per-message
decisions.reasoning: Per-message ContainsKey adds 1-5ms latency per event. Per-batch check (before FindWaitingMessages) balances responsiveness (batch-level granularity) with performance (single check per ~10 messages).

milestones.name: Fix scheduler return-vs-continue bugs
requirements: Scheduler loops continue processing remaining entries when subscription is nil
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests verify both checkOpenCircuitBreakers and checkRepublishingEntries process all entries even when some subscriptions are nil

code_intents.behavior: Change return to continue at line 98 in checkOpenCircuitBreakers when subscription is nil, allowing loop to process remaining circuit breaker entries
Change return to continue at line 131 in checkRepublishingEntries when subscription is nil, allowing loop to process remaining republishing entries
Add unit test verifying checkOpenCircuitBreakers continues processing after nil subscription (multiple CB entries, subscription exists for only some)
Add unit test verifying checkRepublishingEntries continues processing after nil subscription (multiple republishing entries, subscription exists for only some)

milestones.name: Replace local cancel map with Hazelcast distributed checks
requirements: Cancellation signals visible across all Hazelcast replicas
acceptance_criteria: Integration test verifies ForceDelete on one replica stops goroutines on different replica via ContainsKey check

code_intents.behavior: Remove subscriptionCancelMap (line 47), cancelMapMutex (line 48), SetCancelStatus function (lines 56-60), and GetCancelStatus function (lines 62-66)

Update RepublishPendingEvents signature to accept context.Context as first parameter. Replace SetCancelStatus(false) call at line 138 with no-op (distributed signal handled by entry existence). Replace GetCancelStatus checks at lines 142, 173, 183 with RepublishingCache.ContainsKey checks (inverted logic: entry exists = continue, entry missing = cancelled). Check ContainsKey once per batch before FindWaitingMessages/FindProcessedMessagesByDeliveryTypeSSE, not in throttle sleep loop.
Remove SetCancelStatus calls at lines 88, 119, 163, 198, 241 (distributed cancellation now handled by entry deletion alone)
Remove SetCancelStatus call at line 188 in forceDeleteRepublishingEntry (entry deletion sufficient for cancellation signal)
Remove SetCancelStatus call at line 95 (entry deletion at line 91 provides cancellation signal)

Add integration test: two concurrent HandleRepublishingEntry goroutines on different mock replicas, ForceDelete called mid-processing, verify both goroutines detect cancellation via ContainsKey returning false

milestones.name: Add missing entry check in SSE-to-callback listener
requirements: Listener only sets cancel status when RepublishingCache entry exists
acceptance_criteria: Unit test verifies handleDeliveryTypeChangeFromSSEToCallback handles missing entry without error

code_intents.behavior: Add RepublishingCache.Get check before SetCancelStatus(true) at line 119 in handleDeliveryTypeChangeFromCallbackToSSE. Only set cancel status if entry exists. Prevents race where goroutine deletes entry between listener check and cancel signal.
Add unit test: handleDeliveryTypeChangeFromSSEToCallback called when no RepublishingCache entry exists, verify no error and no cancel status set

milestones.name: Replace TryLockWithTimeout with TryLockWithLeaseAndTimeout
requirements: Handler and republishing locks auto-release after 60 seconds
preventing orphaned locks on process crash
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests verify locks acquired with TryLockWithLeaseAndTimeout auto-release after 60s when Unlock not called

code_intents.behavior: Replace TryLockWithTimeout at line 23 with TryLockWithLeaseAndTimeout(ctx, cache.DeliveringLockKey, 60*time.Second, 100*time.Millisecond) to add 60-second automatic lease expiration
Replace TryLockWithTimeout at line 24 with TryLockWithLeaseAndTimeout(ctx, cache.FailedLockKey, 60*time.Second, 100*time.Millisecond) to add 60-second automatic lease expiration
Replace TryLockWithTimeout at line 42 with TryLockWithLeaseAndTimeout(ctx, cache.WaitingLockKey, 60*time.Second, 100*time.Millisecond) to add 60-second automatic lease expiration
Replace TryLockWithTimeout at line 82 with TryLockWithLeaseAndTimeout(ctx, subscriptionId, 60*time.Second, 100*time.Millisecond) to add 60-second automatic lease expiration
Add unit test: verify CheckDeliveringEvents acquires lock with lease, simulate process crash (no Unlock call), verify lock auto-releases after 60 seconds using mock HandlerCache
Add unit test: verify CheckFailedEvents acquires lock with lease, simulate process crash (no Unlock call), verify lock auto-releases after 60 seconds using mock HandlerCache
Add unit test: verify CheckWaitingEvents acquires lock with lease, simulate process crash (no Unlock call), verify lock auto-releases after 60 seconds using mock HandlerCache
